title: Oberna alpina
intro: Oberna alpina, also known as Mountain Oberna, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the family Plumbaginaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, contributing to its use in treating various health issues such as wounds, digestive problems, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Oberna alpina include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to possess significant pharmacological properties. Oberna alpina is commonly used to prepare teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat colds, fever, and digestive issues, and are also employed as a topical treatment for skin irritations and wounds.
taxonomy: Oberna alpina is classified within the family Plantaginaceae. This classification places it within the order Lamiales. At the family level, it is closely related to other genera such as Veronica and Plantago. The classification of Oberna alpina is subject to ongoing research and potential revisions. Further study is necessary to confirm its exact classification.
